1→import { useState } from 'react'
     2→import { Link, useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→import {
     4→  Container,
     5→  Title,
     6→  Text,
     7→  TextInput,
     8→  NativeSelect,
     9→  SimpleGrid,
    10→  Group,
    11→  Stack,
    12→  Card,
    13→  Box,
    14→} from '@mantine/core'
    15→import { Search, BarChart3, Filter, Bookmark } from 'lucide-react'
    16→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
    17→import { US_STATES, SampleSearchCarousel } from '@/components/search'
    18→
    19→const RANKING_OPTIONS = [
    20→  { value: '', label: 'Any rank' },
    21→  { value: '25', label: 'Top 25' },
    22→  { value: '50', label: 'Top 50' },
    23→  { value: '100', label: 'Top 100' },
    24→  { value: '200', label: 'Top 200' },
    25→]
    26→
    27→const features = [
    28→  {
    29→    icon: BarChart3,
    30→    title: 'Comprehensive Data',
    31→    description:
    32→      'Access detailed information on thousands of colleges — from acceptance rates and SAT scores to net price and graduation outcomes.',
    33→  },
    34→  {
    35→    icon: Filter,
    36→    title: 'Faceted Search',
    37→    description:
    38→      'Narrow your list by dozens of criteria at once. Combine filters across academics, cost, location, size, and campus culture.',
    39→  },
    40→  {
    41→    icon: Bookmark,
    42→    title: 'Save & Compare',
    43→    description:
    44→      'Build your personal college list, save searches, and compare schools side by side to make confident decisions.',
    45→  },
    46→]
    47→
    48→export function Home() {
    49→  const [name, setName] = useState('')
    50→  const [state, setState] = useState('')
    51→  const [rankMax, setRankMax] = useState('')
    52→  const [major, setMajor] = useState('')
    53→  const navigate = useNavigate()
    54→
    55→  function buildSearchParams(): URLSearchParams {
    56→    const params = new URLSearchParams()
    57→    if (name.trim()) params.set('q', name.trim())
    58→    if (state) params.set('state', state)
    59→    if (rankMax) params.set('rank_us_news_national', rankMax)
    60→    if (major.trim()) params.set('fields_of_study', major.trim())
    61→    return params
    62→  }
    63→
    64→  function handleSearch(e: React.FormEvent) {
    65→    e.preventDefault()
    66→    const params = buildSearchParams()
    67→    const qs = params.toString()
    68→    navigate(qs ? `/search?${qs}` : '/search')
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  const moreFiltersParams = buildSearchParams()
    72→  const moreFiltersHref = moreFiltersParams.toString()
    73→    ? `/search?${moreFiltersParams.toString()}`
    74→    : '/search'
    75→
    76→  return (
    77→    <main style={{ display: 'flex', flex: 1, flexDirection: 'column' }}>
    78→      {/* ── Hero ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */}
    79→      <Box component="section" bg="gray.0" py={{ base: 40, sm: 60, md: 80 }} px="md">
    80→        <Container size="sm" style={{ textAlign: 'center' }}>
    81→          <Title order={1} mb="sm" c="brand.9" fz={{ base: 36, sm: 48, lg: 60 }}>
    82→            College Power Search
    83→          </Title>
    84→          <Text size="lg" c="dimmed" mb="xl">
    85→            Find your perfect college — by academics, cost, location, athletics, and more.
    86→          </Text>
    87→
    88→          <form onSubmit={handleSearch}>
    89→            <Stack gap="md">
    90→              {/* Name search */}
    91→              <TextInput
    92→                type="search"
    93→                placeholder="College name…"
    94→                value={name}
    95→                onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
    96→                leftSection={<Search size={16} />}
    97→                size="lg"
    98→              />
    99→
   100→              {/* Facet row */}
   101→              <SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 3 }} spacing="sm">
   102→                <NativeSelect
   103→                  label="State"
   104→                  value={state}
   105→                  onChange={(e) => setState(e.target.value)}
   106→                  data={[
   107→                    { value: '', label: 'Any state' },
   108→                    ...US_STATES.map((s) => ({ value: s.value, label: s.label })),
   109→                  ]}
   110→                />
   111→                <NativeSelect
   112→                  label="Ranking"
   113→                  value={rankMax}
   114→                  onChange={(e) => setRankMax(e.target.value)}
   115→                  data={RANKING_OPTIONS}
   116→                />
   117→                <TextInput
   118→                  label="Major"
   119→                  placeholder="e.g. Biology"
   120→                  value={major}
   121→                  onChange={(e) => setMajor(e.target.value)}
   122→                />
   123→              </SimpleGrid>
   124→
   125→              {/* Actions */}
   126→              <Group justify="space-between">
   127→                <Link
   128→                  to={moreFiltersHref}
   129→                  style={{
   130→                    fontSize: '0.875rem',
   131→                    fontWeight: 500,
   132→                    color: 'var(--color-accent)',
   133→                    textDecoration: 'none',
   134→                  }}
   135→                >
   136→                  More filters ›
   137→                </Link>
   138→                <Button type="submit" size="lg" variant="accent">
   139→                  Search
   140→                </Button>
   141→              </Group>
   142→            </Stack>
   143→          </form>
   144→        </Container>
   145→      </Box>
   146→
   147→      {/* ── Sample searches ─────────────────────────────────────────────── */}
   148→      <SampleSearchCarousel />
   149→
   150→      {/* ── Why CPS? ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── */}
   151→      <Box component="section" bg="gray.0" py="xl" px="md">
   152→        <Container size="lg">
   153→          <Title order={2} ta="center" mb="xl" c="brand.9">
   154→            Why College Power Search?
   155→          </Title>
   156→
   157→          <SimpleGrid cols={{ base: 1, sm: 3 }} spacing="lg">
   158→            {features.map(({ icon: Icon, title, description }) => (
   159→              <Card key={title} shadow="sm" withBorder>
   160→                <Box
   161→                  mb="sm"
   162→                  style={{
   163→                    display: 'inline-flex',
   164→                    alignItems: 'center',
   165→                    justifyContent: 'center',
   166→                    width: 44,
   167→                    height: 44,
   168→                    borderRadius: 8,
   169→                    backgroundColor: 'var(--color-primary)',
   170→                  }}
   171→                >
   172→                  <Icon size={20} color="#fff" />
   173→                </Box>
   174→                <Text fw={600} size="lg" c="brand.9" mb="xs">
   175→                  {title}
   176→                </Text>
   177→                <Text size="sm" c="dimmed" lh={1.625}>
   178→                  {description}
   179→                </Text>
   180→              </Card>
   181→            ))}
   182→          </SimpleGrid>
   183→        </Container>
   184→      </Box>
   185→    </main>
   186→  )
   187→}
   188→

1→enum FieldVisibility {
     2→  VISIBLE
     3→  RESTRICTED
     4→  HIDDEN
     5→}
     6→
     7→enum FieldSearchability {
     8→  SEARCHABLE
     9→  RESTRICTED
    10→  NOT_SEARCHABLE
    11→}
    12→
    13→enum Theme {
    14→  CORE
    15→  LOCATION
    16→  ACADEMICS
    17→  COST
    18→  OUTCOMES
    19→  RANKING
    20→  ADMISSIONS
    21→  ATHLETICS
    22→  FIELDS_OF_STUDY
    23→  CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT
    24→  WEATHER
    25→  PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT
    26→  DEMOGRAPHICS
    27→  REPUTATION
    28→  SAFETY
    29→  STUDENT_LIFE
    30→  METADATA
    31→  OTHER
    32→}
    33→
    34→# Shared fields on every search field type
    35→interface SearchField {
    36→  name: String!
    37→  label: String!
    38→  description: String!
    39→  theme: Theme!
    40→  visibility: FieldVisibility!
    41→  searchability: FieldSearchability!
    42→  exportable: Boolean!
    43→  dataSourceID: String
    44→}
    45→
    46→# value is what is sent in filter payloads; displayName is shown to the user
    47→type SearchFieldOption {
    48→  value: String!
    49→  displayName: String!
    50→}
    51→
    52→enum SearchFieldUnit {
    53→  DOLLARS
    54→  PERCENTAGE
    55→  MILES
    56→  HOURS
    57→  MINUTES
    58→}
    59→
    60→# Full-text, substring, or exact-string text filter
    61→type TextSearchField implements SearchField {
    62→  name: String!
    63→  label: String!
    64→  description: String!
    65→  theme: Theme!
    66→  visibility: FieldVisibility!
    67→  searchability: FieldSearchability!
    68→  exportable: Boolean!
    69→  dataSourceID: String
    70→  minLength: Int
    71→  maxLength: Int
    72→  pattern: String
    73→}
    74→
    75→# Boolean toggle — checked sends value "1"; unchecked omits the filter entirely
    76→type BinarySearchField implements SearchField {
    77→  name: String!
    78→  label: String!
    79→  description: String!
    80→  theme: Theme!
    81→  visibility: FieldVisibility!
    82→  searchability: FieldSearchability!
    83→  exportable: Boolean!
    84→  dataSourceID: String
    85→}
    86→
    87→# Single- or multi-select from a fixed option list
    88→type ChoiceSearchField implements SearchField {
    89→  name: String!
    90→  label: String!
    91→  description: String!
    92→  theme: Theme!
    93→  visibility: FieldVisibility!
    94→  searchability: FieldSearchability!
    95→  exportable: Boolean!
    96→  dataSourceID: String
    97→  options: [SearchFieldOption!]!
    98→  minSelections: Int!
    99→  maxSelections: Int
   100→}
   101→
   102→# Single exact numeric value
   103→type NumericalSearchField implements SearchField {
   104→  name: String!
   105→  label: String!
   106→  description: String!
   107→  theme: Theme!
   108→  visibility: FieldVisibility!
   109→  searchability: FieldSearchability!
   110→  exportable: Boolean!
   111→  dataSourceID: String
   112→  min: Float

file.content:
enum FieldVisibility {
  VISIBLE
  RESTRICTED
  HIDDEN
}

enum FieldSearchability {
  SEARCHABLE
  RESTRICTED
  NOT_SEARCHABLE
}

enum Theme {
  CORE
  LOCATION
  ACADEMICS
  COST
  OUTCOMES
  RANKING
  ADMISSIONS
  ATHLETICS
  FIELDS_OF_STUDY
  CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT
  WEATHER
  PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT
  DEMOGRAPHICS
  REPUTATION
  SAFETY
  STUDENT_LIFE
  METADATA
  OTHER
}

# Shared fields on every search field type
interface SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# value is what is sent in filter payloads; displayName is shown to the user
type SearchFieldOption {
  value: String!
  displayName: String!
}

enum SearchFieldUnit {
  DOLLARS
  PERCENTAGE
  MILES
  HOURS
  MINUTES
}

# Full-text, substring, or exact-string text filter
type TextSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  minLength: Int
  maxLength: Int
  pattern: String
}

# Boolean toggle — checked sends value "1"; unchecked omits the filter entirely
type BinarySearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# Single- or multi-select from a fixed option list
type ChoiceSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  options: [SearchFieldOption!]!
  minSelections: Int!
  maxSelections: Int
}

# Single exact numeric value
type NumericalSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultValue: Float
}

# Numeric lower–upper range pair
type NumericalRangeSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultLower: Float
  defaultUpper: Float
  minSpan: Float
  maxSpan: Float
}

# Geo proximity / bounding-box filter
type GeoSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  defaultLatitude: Float
  defaultLongitude: Float
  maxRadiusMiles: Float!
}

extend type Query {
  searchFields: [SearchField!]!
  searchOutputFields: [SearchOutputField!]!
}

# Describes a field that appears in search result rows. Shares name/label/
# description/visibility/exportable with the corresponding SearchField where
# one exists; adds display-specific attributes (defaultVisible, sortable).
type SearchOutputField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  # defaultVisible: true = shown by default in the results grid;
  # false = available in the column picker but hidden by default.
  defaultVisible: Boolean!
  # sortable: true = the column header shows a sort control.
  sortable: Boolean!
  # unit: optional hint for how to format the displayed value.
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  # outputKey: dot-separated path into the data JSONB blob for extracting
  # the field's value from SearchInstitution.data. Null for fields that are
  # direct properties on SearchInstitution (name, city, state, etc.).
  outputKey: String
}

    14→// Connect creates a pgxpool.Pool, pings the database, and returns the pool.
    15→// It retries up to maxConnectAttempts times with exponential backoff.
    16→func Connect(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error) {
    17→	cfg, err := pgxpool.ParseConfig(databaseURL)
    18→	if err != nil {
    19→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("db: failed to parse database URL: %w", err)
    20→	}
    21→
    22→	var pool *pgxpool.Pool
    23→	for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxConnectAttempts; attempt++ {
    24→		pool, err = pgxpool.NewWithConfig(ctx, cfg)
    25→		if err != nil {
    26→			slog.Warn("db: connect attempt failed", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
    27→			time.Sleep(time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second)
    28→			continue
    29→		}
    30→
    31→		if err = pool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
    32→			pool.Close()
    33→			slog.Warn("db: ping attempt failed", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
    34→			time.Sleep(time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second)
    35→			continue
    36→		}
    37→
    38→		stat := pool.Stat()
    39→		slog.Info("db: connected",
    40→			"attempt", attempt,
    41→			"max_conns", stat.MaxConns(),
    42→		)
    43→		return pool, nil
    44→	}
    45→
    46→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("db: failed to connect after %d attempts: %w", maxConnectAttempts, err)
    47→}

input.content:
# Fix: Fields of Study Facet (kapow-n0a9)

## Context

The "fields of study" search facet is non-functional. The backend field exists (`fields_of_study` MultiEnumField in registry.go:882) and SQL generation works (`nces_fos ?| $1::text[]`), but the field is registered with `Options: nil` (open-ended). The API returns `options: []`, so the `MultiSelectCheckboxes` UI component renders nothing to interact with.

The `search_base.cip_data` table (~2000+ rows) has CIP codes with descriptions and credential levels — this is the data source for populating the facet. The Home page also has a free-text "Major" input that sets `fields_of_study` as raw text, which won't work with CIP-code-based filtering.

**Note:** `program_percentage_*` and `program_bachelors_*` fields do NOT exist in the registry — no removal needed.

## Approach

Add a `cipCodes` GraphQL query for search-as-you-type lookup against `cip_data` (Bachelor's only). Build a new `CipCodeSelect` frontend component that queries this endpoint and renders an async multi-select. Wire it into FilterControl for the `fields_of_study` field.

---

## Steps

### 1. Add GraphQL schema for CIP code queries

**File:** `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql`

```graphql
type CipCode {
  code: String!
  description: String!
}

extend type Query {
  cipCodes(search: String!, limit: Int): [CipCode!]!
  cipCodesByCode(codes: [String!]!): [CipCode!]!
}
```

- `cipCodes`: search by description or code substring, filtered to Bachelor's level, default limit 20
- `cipCodesByCode`: reverse lookup for restoring selections from URL params

### 2. Regenerate gqlgen code

```bash
cd api && make generate
```

### 3. Implement resolvers

**File:** `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers.go`

`CipCodes` resolver:
- Return empty if `search` < 2 chars
- Default limit to 20, cap at 50
- SQL: `SELECT DISTINCT code, description FROM search_base.cip_data WHERE level = 'BACHELOR' AND (description ILIKE '%' || $1 || '%' OR code ILIKE '%' || $1 || '%') ORDER BY description LIMIT $2`

`CipCodesByCode` resolver:
- Return empty if `codes` is empty
- SQL: `SELECT DISTINCT code, description FROM search_base.cip_data WHERE level = 'BACHELOR' AND code = ANY($1::text[]) ORDER BY description`

Both use `r.DB.Query(ctx, sql, args...)` with parameterized queries.

### 4. Backend tests

**File:** `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers_test.go` (new or extend existing)

- Unit test: `CipCodes` returns empty for short search strings
- Unit test: `CipCodes` caps limit at 50
- Unit test: `CipCodesByCode` returns empty for empty codes slice

Note: Integration tests would need a live DB with cip_data loaded — skip for now as the SQL is straightforward and cip_data is a simple lookup table.

### 5. Schema sync

```bash
make web-schema-sync
```

Regenerates `web/schema.graphql` and `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts`.

### 6. Create CipCodeSelect component

**File:** `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.tsx` (new)

Behavior:
- Text input with 400ms debounce (matching existing `DebouncedTextInput` pattern)
- When input >= 2 chars, fires `CipCodesQuery` via `useQuery` with `pause: searchText.length < 2`
- Results shown as scrollable checkbox list (same visual style as `MultiSelectCheckboxes`)
- Selected items displayed as removable tags above the search input
- On mount with existing URL values, fires `CipCodesByCodeQuery` to resolve descriptions
- Emits `{ values: string[] }` via `onChange` (CIP codes only)

Props: `{ field: SearchField, value: unknown, onChange: (value: unknown | undefined) => void, source?: DataSource }`

Uses `graphql()` from `gql.tada` for both queries (mandatory per web/CLAUDE.md).

### 7. Wire into FilterControl

**File:** `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx`

Add dispatch before the generic `ChoiceSearchField` handler (line 375):

```tsx
if (field.__typename === 'ChoiceSearchField' && field.name === 'fields_of_study') {
  return <CipCodeSelect field={field} value={value} onChange={onChange} source={source} />
}
```

### 8. Update Home page major input

**File:** `web/src/pages/Home.tsx`

Remove the free-text "Major" `TextInput` and its state. The fields_of_study filter now requires CIP codes which aren't suitable for a quick-search hero form. The search page sidebar provides the proper CipCodeSelect. Keep the 3-column grid with State and Ranking only (or 2-column).

### 9. Update barrel export

**File:** `web/src/components/search/index.ts`

Add `export { CipCodeSelect } from './CipCodeSelect'`.

### 10. Frontend tests

**File:** `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.test.tsx` (new)

- Renders search input with field label
- Shows results when query returns data (mock useQuery)
- Selecting a result calls onChange with `{ values: [code] }`
- Removing a selected item updates values
- Shows "No results" message when search returns empty

### 11. Quality gate

```bash
make test-standard   # Go unit + TS build + ESLint + web unit tests
```

---

## Files changed

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql` | Add CipCode type + 2 queries |
| `api/graph/generated/generated.go` | Regenerated |
| `api/graph/model/models_gen.go` | Regenerated |
| `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers.go` | Add CipCodes + CipCodesByCode resolvers |
| `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers_test.go` | Add resolver unit tests |
| `web/schema.graphql` | Regenerated |
| `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts` | Regenerated |
| `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.tsx` | New: async search multi-select |
| `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.test.tsx` | New: component tests |
| `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx` | Add fields_of_study dispatch |
| `web/src/components/search/index.ts` | Export CipCodeSelect |
| `web/src/pages/Home.tsx` | Remove Major text input |

## Verification

1. `make test-standard` passes (Go unit + TS build + lint + web unit tests)
2. Manual: search page shows "Fields of Study" facet with search input, typing "Biology" shows matching CIP codes, selecting one filters results
3. URL contains CIP codes (e.g. `fields_of_study=26.0101`), page reload restores selections with descriptions

input.plan:
# Fix: Fields of Study Facet (kapow-n0a9)

## Context

The "fields of study" search facet is non-functional. The backend field exists (`fields_of_study` MultiEnumField in registry.go:882) and SQL generation works (`nces_fos ?| $1::text[]`), but the field is registered with `Options: nil` (open-ended). The API returns `options: []`, so the `MultiSelectCheckboxes` UI component renders nothing to interact with.

The `search_base.cip_data` table (~2000+ rows) has CIP codes with descriptions and credential levels — this is the data source for populating the facet. The Home page also has a free-text "Major" input that sets `fields_of_study` as raw text, which won't work with CIP-code-based filtering.

**Note:** `program_percentage_*` and `program_bachelors_*` fields do NOT exist in the registry — no removal needed.

## Approach

Add a `cipCodes` GraphQL query for search-as-you-type lookup against `cip_data` (Bachelor's only). Build a new `CipCodeSelect` frontend component that queries this endpoint and renders an async multi-select. Wire it into FilterControl for the `fields_of_study` field.

---

## Steps

### 1. Add GraphQL schema for CIP code queries

**File:** `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql`

```graphql
type CipCode {
  code: String!
  description: String!
}

extend type Query {
  cipCodes(search: String!, limit: Int): [CipCode!]!
  cipCodesByCode(codes: [String!]!): [CipCode!]!
}
```

- `cipCodes`: search by description or code substring, filtered to Bachelor's level, default limit 20
- `cipCodesByCode`: reverse lookup for restoring selections from URL params

### 2. Regenerate gqlgen code

```bash
cd api && make generate
```

### 3. Implement resolvers

**File:** `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers.go`

`CipCodes` resolver:
- Return empty if `search` < 2 chars
- Default limit to 20, cap at 50
- SQL: `SELECT DISTINCT code, description FROM search_base.cip_data WHERE level = 'BACHELOR' AND (description ILIKE '%' || $1 || '%' OR code ILIKE '%' || $1 || '%') ORDER BY description LIMIT $2`

`CipCodesByCode` resolver:
- Return empty if `codes` is empty
- SQL: `SELECT DISTINCT code, description FROM search_base.cip_data WHERE level = 'BACHELOR' AND code = ANY($1::text[]) ORDER BY description`

Both use `r.DB.Query(ctx, sql, args...)` with parameterized queries.

### 4. Backend tests

**File:** `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers_test.go` (new or extend existing)

- Unit test: `CipCodes` returns empty for short search strings
- Unit test: `CipCodes` caps limit at 50
- Unit test: `CipCodesByCode` returns empty for empty codes slice

Note: Integration tests would need a live DB with cip_data loaded — skip for now as the SQL is straightforward and cip_data is a simple lookup table.

### 5. Schema sync

```bash
make web-schema-sync
```

Regenerates `web/schema.graphql` and `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts`.

### 6. Create CipCodeSelect component

**File:** `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.tsx` (new)

Behavior:
- Text input with 400ms debounce (matching existing `DebouncedTextInput` pattern)
- When input >= 2 chars, fires `CipCodesQuery` via `useQuery` with `pause: searchText.length < 2`
- Results shown as scrollable checkbox list (same visual style as `MultiSelectCheckboxes`)
- Selected items displayed as removable tags above the search input
- On mount with existing URL values, fires `CipCodesByCodeQuery` to resolve descriptions
- Emits `{ values: string[] }` via `onChange` (CIP codes only)

Props: `{ field: SearchField, value: unknown, onChange: (value: unknown | undefined) => void, source?: DataSource }`

Uses `graphql()` from `gql.tada` for both queries (mandatory per web/CLAUDE.md).

### 7. Wire into FilterControl

**File:** `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx`

Add dispatch before the generic `ChoiceSearchField` handler (line 375):

```tsx
if (field.__typename === 'ChoiceSearchField' && field.name === 'fields_of_study') {
  return <CipCodeSelect field={field} value={value} onChange={onChange} source={source} />
}
```

### 8. Update Home page major input

**File:** `web/src/pages/Home.tsx`

Remove the free-text "Major" `TextInput` and its state. The fields_of_study filter now requires CIP codes which aren't suitable for a quick-search hero form. The search page sidebar provides the proper CipCodeSelect. Keep the 3-column grid with State and Ranking only (or 2-column).

### 9. Update barrel export

**File:** `web/src/components/search/index.ts`

Add `export { CipCodeSelect } from './CipCodeSelect'`.

### 10. Frontend tests

**File:** `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.test.tsx` (new)

- Renders search input with field label
- Shows results when query returns data (mock useQuery)
- Selecting a result calls onChange with `{ values: [code] }`
- Removing a selected item updates values
- Shows "No results" message when search returns empty

### 11. Quality gate

```bash
make test-standard   # Go unit + TS build + ESLint + web unit tests
```

---

## Files changed

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql` | Add CipCode type + 2 queries |
| `api/graph/generated/generated.go` | Regenerated |
| `api/graph/model/models_gen.go` | Regenerated |
| `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers.go` | Add CipCodes + CipCodesByCode resolvers |
| `api/graph/resolver/search_fields.resolvers_test.go` | Add resolver unit tests |
| `web/schema.graphql` | Regenerated |
| `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts` | Regenerated |
| `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.tsx` | New: async search multi-select |
| `web/src/components/search/CipCodeSelect.test.tsx` | New: component tests |
| `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx` | Add fields_of_study dispatch |
| `web/src/components/search/index.ts` | Export CipCodeSelect |
| `web/src/pages/Home.tsx` | Remove Major text input |

## Verification

1. `make test-standard` passes (Go unit + TS build + lint + web unit tests)
2. Manual: search page shows "Fields of Study" facet with search input, typing "Biology" shows matching CIP codes, selecting one filters results
3. URL contains CIP codes (e.g. `fields_of_study=26.0101`), page reload restores selections with descriptions

file.content:
enum FieldVisibility {
  VISIBLE
  RESTRICTED
  HIDDEN
}

enum FieldSearchability {
  SEARCHABLE
  RESTRICTED
  NOT_SEARCHABLE
}

enum Theme {
  CORE
  LOCATION
  ACADEMICS
  COST
  OUTCOMES
  RANKING
  ADMISSIONS
  ATHLETICS
  FIELDS_OF_STUDY
  CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT
  WEATHER
  PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT
  DEMOGRAPHICS
  REPUTATION
  SAFETY
  STUDENT_LIFE
  METADATA
  OTHER
}

# Shared fields on every search field type
interface SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# value is what is sent in filter payloads; displayName is shown to the user
type SearchFieldOption {
  value: String!
  displayName: String!
}

enum SearchFieldUnit {
  DOLLARS
  PERCENTAGE
  MILES
  HOURS
  MINUTES
}

# Full-text, substring, or exact-string text filter
type TextSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  minLength: Int
  maxLength: Int
  pattern: String
}

# Boolean toggle — checked sends value "1"; unchecked omits the filter entirely
type BinarySearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# Single- or multi-select from a fixed option list
type ChoiceSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  options: [SearchFieldOption!]!
  minSelections: Int!
  maxSelections: Int
}

# Single exact numeric value
type NumericalSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultValue: Float
}

# Numeric lower–upper range pair
type NumericalRangeSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultLower: Float
  defaultUpper: Float
  minSpan: Float
  maxSpan: Float
}

# Geo proximity / bounding-box filter
type GeoSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  defaultLatitude: Float
  defaultLongitude: Float
  maxRadiusMiles: Float!
}

extend type Query {
  searchFields: [SearchField!]!
  searchOutputFields: [SearchOutputField!]!
}

# Describes a field that appears in search result rows. Shares name/label/
# description/visibility/exportable with the corresponding SearchField where
# one exists; adds display-specific attributes (defaultVisible, sortable).
type SearchOutputField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  # defaultVisible: true = shown by default in the results grid;
  # false = available in the column picker but hidden by default.
  defaultVisible: Boolean!
  # sortable: true = the column header shows a sort control.
  sortable: Boolean!
  # unit: optional hint for how to format the displayed value.
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  # outputKey: dot-separated path into the data JSONB blob for extracting
  # the field's value from SearchInstitution.data. Null for fields that are
  # direct properties on SearchInstitution (name, city, state, etc.).
  outputKey: String
}

enum FieldVisibility {
  VISIBLE
  RESTRICTED
  HIDDEN
}

enum FieldSearchability {
  SEARCHABLE
  RESTRICTED
  NOT_SEARCHABLE
}

enum Theme {
  CORE
  LOCATION
  ACADEMICS
  COST
  OUTCOMES
  RANKING
  ADMISSIONS
  ATHLETICS
  FIELDS_OF_STUDY
  CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT
  WEATHER
  PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT
  DEMOGRAPHICS
  REPUTATION
  SAFETY
  STUDENT_LIFE
  METADATA
  OTHER
}

# Shared fields on every search field type
interface SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# value is what is sent in filter payloads; displayName is shown to the user
type SearchFieldOption {
  value: String!
  displayName: String!
}

enum SearchFieldUnit {
  DOLLARS
  PERCENTAGE
  MILES
  HOURS
  MINUTES
}

# Full-text, substring, or exact-string text filter
type TextSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  minLength: Int
  maxLength: Int
  pattern: String
}

# Boolean toggle — checked sends value "1"; unchecked omits the filter entirely
type BinarySearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# Single- or multi-select from a fixed option list
type ChoiceSearchField implements SearchField {

}

### Step 5: Create CipCodeSelect component

Let me check the existing imports and patterns first:

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Strict GraphQL utilities — re-exports urql hooks with types that reject raw strings.
     3→ *
     4→ * Import useQuery/useMutation from here instead of 'urql'. Raw string queries
     5→ * bypass gql.tada's compile-time schema validation, which has caused production
     6→ * bugs (e.g. querying fields that don't exist in the schema).
     7→ *
     8→ * These wrappers require a parsed DocumentNode (has 'kind' and 'definitions'),
     9→ * which gql.tada's graphql() returns but raw strings do not satisfy.
    10→ *
    11→ * Usage:
    12→ *   import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
    13→ *   import { useQuery, useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'
    14→ *
    15→ *   const MyQuery = graphql(`query MyQuery { ... }`)
    16→ *   const [result] = useQuery({ query: MyQuery })
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import {
    20→  useQuery as _useQuery,
    21→  useMutation as _useMutation,
    22→  type UseQueryResponse,
    23→  type UseMutationResponse,
    24→  type AnyVariables,
    25→  type OperationContext,
    26→  type RequestPolicy,
    27→} from 'urql'
    28→
    29→// A parsed GraphQL document has { kind: 'Document', definitions: [...] }.
    30→// gql.tada's graphql() returns this at runtime. Raw strings don't have these
    31→// properties, so TypeScript rejects them.
    32→interface ParsedDocument {
    33→  readonly kind: string
    34→  readonly definitions: readonly unknown[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→interface StrictQueryArgs<Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables> {
    38→  query: ParsedDocument
    39→  variables?: Variables
    40→  requestPolicy?: RequestPolicy
    41→  context?: Partial<OperationContext>
    42→  pause?: boolean
    43→}
    44→
    45→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    46→export function useQuery<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    47→  args: StrictQueryArgs<Variables>,
    48→): UseQueryResponse<Data, Variables> {
    49→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    50→  return _useQuery(args as any)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    54→export function useMutation<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    55→  query: ParsedDocument,
    56→): UseMutationResponse<Data, Variables> {
    57→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    58→  return _useMutation(query as any)
    59→}
    60→

    24→beforeEach(() => {

     2→import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'

    14→    selector({ homeLatitude: null, homeLongitude: null, homeState: null, loaded: false, loading: false, load: vi.fn(), update: vi.fn(), clear: vi.fn() }),
    15→}))
    16→

    20→  return {

    23→  }

    60→    expect(screen.getByText('Save & Compare')).toBeInTheDocument()
    61→  })

input.content:
Task kapow-n0a9 (Fix fields of study facet) is complete. Branch `worktree/fix-fos-facet` pushed.

## What changed

**Backend:**
- Added `CipCode` GraphQL type and two queries: `cipCodes(search, limit)` for search-as-you-type and `cipCodesByCode(codes)` for reverse lookup from URL
- Both queries filter `search_base.cip_data` to Bachelor's level only
- `cipCodes` requires 2+ char search, defaults to 20 results (max 50)

**Frontend:**
- New `CipCodeSelect` component: debounced search input → async CIP code results → checkbox selection → removable tags
- Wired into `FilterControl` for the `fields_of_study` field (dispatches before generic ChoiceSearchField handler)
- URL restoration: on page load with existing CIP codes in URL, fires `cipCodesByCode` to resolve descriptions for display
- Removed free-text "Major" input from Home page (incompatible with CIP-code-based filtering)
- Updated Home page tests to reflect removal

**Files changed:** 13 files (3 new, 10 modified)

**Note:** `program_percentage_*` and `program_bachelors_*` fields were NOT found in the registry — they don't exist, so no removal was needed.

## Quality gate
`make test-standard` passes: Go unit tests, TS build, ESLint (zero warnings), web unit tests, Go lint — all green.
